さばすち 2022/05/02 23:39
先日投稿した曲(REMIX)です。ステムはボーカルだけ使いました。 00:20~00:29は意図的に音量を小さくしたのですが失敗したのでここは飛ばして大丈夫です。00:46~からスパソとボーカルどちらも大事にしたいという思いで帯域が被ってしまい、ボーカルの良さが消えてしまったように感じますがどうですかね？（ここのアドバイスほしいです、、、） 飛ばし飛ばしで大丈夫なので気になるところや自分だったらこうするみたいなのあれば教えてください！（音楽自体ほんとに久しぶりでいろいろ忘れていたので初歩的なことでも何かあれば、、） (edited)

Blub 2022/05/03 2:19
how do you approach the structure of songs, I always struggle to transition from the beginning to the middle and find that I veer too much off of the theme and make it too complicated. Any tips on structuring of songs?
さばすち
先日投稿した曲(REMIX)です。ステムはボーカルだけ使いました。 00:20~00:29は意図的に音量を小さくしたのですが失敗したのでここは飛ばして大丈夫です。00:46~からスパソとボーカルどちらも大事にしたいという思いで帯域が被ってしまい、ボーカルの良さが消えてしまったように感じますがどうですかね？（ここのアドバイスほしいです、、、） 飛ばし飛ばしで大丈夫なので気になるところや自分だったらこうするみたいなのあれば教えてください！（音楽自体ほんとに久しぶりでいろいろ忘れていたので初歩的なことでも何かあれば、、） (edited)
sober bear (Staff) 2022/05/03 2:37
ぼくだったらですけどphaserとnotch filterでスパソに何かしら動き付けるのとボーカルのメロディに対して美味しいところを生かすようにオートメーション書いたりするかもです、あとはリバーブのmixをオートメーション書いてさらに広がりをコントロールするとスパソが一本調子ではなく動きがある感じになると思います
さばすち 2022/05/03 3:14
notch filterの存在忘れていました… オートメーションも書くべきですよね……励みになります！！ リバーブに関してはｽﾊﾟｿをタイトに聴かせたかったのでほとんど書けてなかったはずです。あえて一本調子にしたのですが、もっと動かした方が確かに楽しくなりそうですね…！ありがとうございます！
1
Blub
how do you approach the structure of songs, I always struggle to transition from the beginning to the middle and find that I veer too much off of the theme and make it too complicated. Any tips on structuring of songs?
uj 2022/05/04 13:24
structuring is something I don't usually think about, since I just make variations
uj
structuring is something I don't usually think about, since I just make variations
Blub 2022/05/04 13:25
That makes a lot of sense, do you don’t ever think about intro, drop/middle or whatever?
uj 2022/05/04 13:27
I usually start with theme or chord progression
13:27
I make chord structure a bit weird, since my song is about that you can listen every track as a song
13:28
so my chords have melody like top notes
13:28
this was also what I wanted to talk about on video too
13:29
you gotta just have a lot of progressions that goes together
uj
I make chord structure a bit weird, since my song is about that you can listen every track as a song

Blub 2022/05/08 12:04
・q1:in the video you show how to do sidechian to bass in your own way, but the window doesnt show because i assume of your recording software. Can you explain how you sidechain to bass again? ・q2: in your video I am noticed a lot of peaking in your sound and the said result of it sounding so loud that it sounds grainy, I also saw your master chain with the Soundsource Reference 4 BELOW that of your voxengo recording software that I know you use for recording the sound in fl studio. My question is if you hear it differently than the exported version given plugin order or do you hear it umcompressed and actually that loud and if so how does it affect your producing experience since its hard to distinguish everything imo ・q3:could you link your wavetables also? I have seen your past livestreams where you use them and I need a good custom wavetable since the serum ones are just meh imo. q4: tier list for plugins for eq/compression and boost for drums? I have some of yours but can you make a list for some that you use for like kick and mid/highs? (edited)
uj 2022/05/08 12:32
@Blub reference is for my output only not for recording
12:32

12:33

12:34
Wavetables are third party or serum default
12:34
Idk where anymore
12:34
It's not named lol
12:34

12:34
Look for forums and such

A few topics that would be awesome for you to go over in the next video 1. chord progression theory and methodology when you produce eg. what chords cause what feelings and why they’re there, 2. Mixing and your process analysis on when and why you put plugins where they are and what you listen for before changing dynamics of things (since I believe most of us struggle with over compression and you do it so amazingly :D)
7:18

7:18

uj 2022/05/18 14:04
Tip : carefully put top notes (edited)
14:04
Of a chord
14:04
Those define the vibe
14:05
When you put higher note on top of the chord, it gets brigter
14:05
And lower, less bright

that's facts

Always make movement

dreamEX 2022/07/11 16:38

16:43
Also that mixing tip about placing new sounds in a different space to audition was huge for me
16:45
Already noticing differences in my workflow
uj
it's so easy with full volume speakers holy shit
spritix 2022/07/11 17:52
watch out to not destroy your ears tho that would be bad lol (edited)
dreamEX
Also that mixing tip about placing new sounds in a different space to audition was huge for me
spritix 2022/07/11 17:53
what was the tip? I guess I wasn't there a the right time to hear it (edited)
spritix
what was the tip? I guess I wasn't there a the right time to hear it (edited)
uj 2022/07/11 18:11
He asked what I would do if the frequency of the sounds are conflicting
18:12
And I answered that Placing it in different stereo position is helpful
18:12
3 dimensional mixing
spritix 2022/07/11 18:12
Oh right I see, thanks
spritix
Oh right I see, thanks
dreamEX 2022/07/11 18:32
Yep. Taking that advice, in situations where i want to further add elements to a section that already had a lot of sound. Instead of muting mixer tracks and losing context, I just temporarily shoved all other sounds towards the middle, which created room on the sides to hear the new stuff
18:33
Makes layering a whole lot easier
uj 2022/07/11 18:48
Unison on synths are best stereo maker (edited)
18:49
I place leads at center with unisoned pads
spritix 2022/07/11 18:50
Yeah same
uj 2022/07/11 18:51
I have so many ways of placing sounds on weirdest places
spritix 2022/07/11 18:51
Btw do you some times copy-paste your leads to make it a bit more stereo or you always put your leads mono?
18:51
lmaoo that's a thing I gotta learn
18:52
'cause I have issues placing sounds really in stereo like full left or full right
spritix
Btw do you some times copy-paste your leads to make it a bit more stereo or you always put your leads mono?
spritix 2022/07/11 18:52
I mean like duplicate it twice, put one left and the other one right and slightly offset it to create stereo (edited)
uj 2022/07/11 18:52
I don't use presets for lead, so depends
18:53
I might place it on some weird place when something like piano is on the way
spritix 2022/07/11 18:53
I see
uj 2022/07/11 18:53
I din't usually pan as well
18:53
Except for percs
spritix 2022/07/11 18:54
Oh yeah? Funny 'cause it sometimes looks like it
18:54
I guess the fact that you always use unison creates that (edited)
uj 2022/07/11 18:54
Also, Haas trick
spritix 2022/07/11 18:54
yessir
18:54
but I got an issue with haas effect
18:55
I always hear the side which the sound comes from first
18:55
And I got the same issue whatever haas plugin I use, except for Ozone Imager
18:56
Do you have a solution for that or it's bound to happen since it's an haas effect? (edited)
spritix
Do you have a solution for that or it's bound to happen since it's an haas effect? (edited)
uj 2022/07/11 19:49

uj
@Tier 3 what would be the best thing right now that I should talk about?
dreamEX 2022/07/11 22:55
Just ideas to throw out with no expectations - some of these are time sinks i know. -Maybe facilitating some sort of peer review stream or video. -Hosting a song creation event like "make with this limitation" or "synthesia from this image/word/song". Maybe not with prizes or winners as it would fuel drama, but just as a fun community thing. -Show more of your workflow and sound design as you work. Even uncommentated i can listen to while im at my job. -Expand on your approach of sound design and mixing of percussion. Especially accoustics like しゃぼん玉とあなたの夢 and 雪の降る街で、あなたを待っている。 -Maybe pick apart a realllllly old song you made and point out some flaws you feel like it has. How you would improve it if you worked on / remade it today etc. (edited)
Memo 2022/07/12 0:58
I second all of these, these are rly good ideas
dreamEX
Just ideas to throw out with no expectations - some of these are time sinks i know. -Maybe facilitating some sort of peer review stream or video. -Hosting a song creation event like "make with this limitation" or "synthesia from this image/word/song". Even uncommentated i can listen to while im at my job. -Expand on your approach of sound design and mixing of percussion. This channel has been a wealth of info for me https://youtube.com/c/inthemix (edited)
In The Mix
Hi I'm Michael Wynne, an audio mastering engineer. In The Mix is all about simplifying the recording, production, mixing and mastering process. No matter which DAW you use or what equipment you have, I'm determined to help you succeed and unlock your creativity. I try to share as much as I can about the industry and give an insight into the w...
1

dreamEX
This channel has been a wealth of info for me https://youtube.com/c/inthemix (edited)
[nxs] 2022/07/18 6:54

dreamEX
What specifically about mixing
[nxs] 2022/07/18 6:55
It doesn't sound coherent
[nxs]
It doesn't sound coherent
dreamEX 2022/07/18 21:23
Well there is a good amount of ear training you have to do through practice that can't be taught. But I can say your mixing will likely improve if you understand types of equalization, compression, saturation, and limiting at a fundamental level.
1
21:25
stereo mixing with mid-side eqing is also important
1
dreamEX 2022/07/18 21:43
Once you know how they work, the application of them will be a bit more apparent.
1

dreamEX 2022/07/21 20:47
Oh cool. Ill go through these at work tonight thanks
20:50
Theres a number of things ive learned from these types of channels that i didnt learn at all from just playing in my daw for 5 years by myself.
20:51
A good guide can go a long way. Sadly they are not all good. Youtube shorts especially.
uj 2022/07/21 20:52
I kinda wanna do these videos myself, of course I cannot speak for engineering standpoint or professional standpoint bc I'm like borderline amateur, but I can talk about what goes through my mind when I'm making music
dreamEX 2022/07/21 20:54
I think your body of work will at least have people interested. Are you talking introductory level stuff?
uj 2022/07/21 20:56
Industry level could go long way since I start doing in my home studios
20:56
dreamEX 2022/07/21 20:58
ive watched a lot of these and even the less insightful ones people seem to like.
20:59
The bar might not be that high. (edited)
dreamEX 2022/07/21 21:29
ultimately, any insight really into how an established producer thinks during their process seems valuable. Even if it isn't the most technical and by the book.
21:31
We live in an age where people don't go to school for it, watch videos online, and learn to make music on somewhat affordable software. If anything, content like that may be more palatable for some. (edited)

[Ableton] Ruh Stephan 2022/08/27 2:22
Just released today!! I’d appreciate some feedback! I tried to keep it simple, but I’d love to know what I can improve on my sampled lo-fi beats!! https://open.spotify.com/track/5TdCaZlGolHq3FVe0yOwoo?si=rJOk-sfORPqvteJxJsxjwQ
1
dreamEX 2022/08/30 3:51
i just clicked play on the embed and realized after like 2 minutes it was a sample loop (edited)
3:52
sometimes lofi beats are actually repetitive like that so i didn't think much of it (edited)
3:55
may be a little sleep deprived
[Ableton] Ruh Stephan 2022/08/31 4:46
Yeah, I think it's a little repetitive too, but I don't know if this is a bad thing or not. Lo-fi works very well as background music, and this lack of variation helps not to draw the listener's attention, but I still thinking I could add more textures, or something more... ngl I think is a lazy production, but it serves its purpose.

may be a little sleep deprived
[Ableton] Ruh Stephan 2022/08/31 4:46

[Ableton] Ruh Stephan

dreamEX 2022/09/04 13:30
agomus (FL) 2022/09/04 15:18
When the piano like 30 cents flat on I Secretly Love U
15:18
How is it so unnoticeable
15:19
But if you compare it to played on a 440hz piano it’s really dissonant until you lower it 30 cents
15:19
Very creative choice works very well actually
[Ableton / Reaper] Fawful 2022/09/04 15:28
432hz is a known possible frequency for A
15:28
people use it basically all the time
15:29
... okay, I'm exaggerating, but it's still a relatively common tuning
15:29
(432 is 31.77c flat from 440)
agomus (FL) 2022/09/04 15:41
So did he use a 432hz piano on purpose or by accident
15:42
Because the bass is still 440
15:42
Yet it sounds fine like they’re both 440 without paying much attention
Luca 2022/09/04 17:13

17:13

[FL] Qsie 2022/09/05 4:17
be prepared for tons of missing plugin errors
4:17
unless you've got cross-compatible VSTs
Blub 2022/09/05 12:04
all i have is vst3 not normal vst ujico uses hella old versions of plugins XD
Blub 2022/09/05 13:14
A few questions I have (actually a lot lol) after going through pixelize... 1. Do you normally use sonarworks enabled? like turning on the power button so its blue, when I do that it personally sounds stale and bland but the normal, enabled version sounds just fine without it enabled 2. When you use vocoder, I assume you take a vocal and put it through the vocoder plugin, but why do you have it panned to the far right side? 3. Do you sidechain on this? If so can you try to explain how you use serum as a sidechain, its too big brain for me to figure out. 4. What is the purpose of MetaPlugin? Never seen it b4 and am curious as to how you use it since it shows up everywhere 5. Very similar to #4 but with MAudioAlign, again it appears in a lot of places and I dont notice any changes when moving knobs around it might just be my own ears not noticing anything. 6. What sample pack did you get your foley from? Its very unique in a lot of respects imo. 7. What do you use pulsar 1178 for? Also with Presswerk, dont have either plugin so if I actually buy it id love to know how you use them. 8. On a side note, I cant hear the pixeloves audio in the mix and only when I disable all the plugins used for it. Not sure why. Dont think its just me. I hope this isnt too much. I really enjoyed this experience... Like REALLY, really enjoyed this. Thanks for all you do!

Blub
A few questions I have (actually a lot lol) after going through pixelize... 1. Do you normally use sonarworks enabled? like turning on the power button so its blue, when I do that it personally sounds stale and bland but the normal, enabled version sounds just fine without it enabled 2. When you use vocoder, I assume you take a vocal and put it through the vocoder plugin, but why do you have it panned to the far right side? 3. Do you sidechain on this? If so can you try to explain how you use serum as a sidechain, its too big brain for me to figure out. 4. What is the purpose of MetaPlugin? Never seen it b4 and am curious as to how you use it since it shows up everywhere 5. Very similar to #4 but with MAudioAlign, again it appears in a lot of places and I dont notice any changes when moving knobs around it might just be my own ears not noticing anything. 6. What sample pack did you get your foley from? Its very unique in a lot of respects imo. 7. What do you use pulsar 1178 for? Also with Presswerk, dont have either plugin so if I actually buy it id love to know how you use them. 8. On a side note, I cant hear the pixeloves audio in the mix and only when I disable all the plugins used for it. Not sure why. Dont think its just me. I hope this isnt too much. I really enjoyed this experience... Like REALLY, really enjoyed this. Thanks for all you do!
Blub 2022/09/07 11:03
if you cant answer all of them maybe just the ones that you think are the most relevant?
uj 2022/09/07 11:24
Just wait for me lol (edited)
11:24
Few (8) questions at once is hard to answer
11:29
I need sonarworks for speakers not headset
11:30
Vocoder is panned because of LR mode , it's vocodex's feature
11:30
I said I'll make a video about sidechaining
11:31
Metaplugin is like patcher, but enables oversampling to any plugins
11:31
Mautoalign is for phase alignment
11:32
Presswerk is more versatile and i use more
11:32
8 is not a problem on my side so idk
11:32
@Blub
11:34
Also, about Mautoalign you can search for yourself
11:34
I think you're about to ask what's phase alignment
11:35

spritix 2022/10/24 1:51
Hey @uj I'm tagging you here 'cause I guess you don't check private messages from strangers I just wanted to let you know that I would love to help on the subtitles of your videos on Patreon. As you may remember I am French, almost bilingual in English and I kinda have a side hobby which is subtitling. I used to do subtitles on youtube videos when the community still was able to do it and I would translate English videos with French subtitles (and also subtitling french videos in French). Anyway, my point is that I'd love to help and add a language to your Patreon videos (or maybe more ?) for your french fans 'cause i know there's a lot, that aren't that good in English and that would need a translation. So if your interested, I am too and would love to help and my dms are open lol Have a great day PS: Restart MV was fucking amazing, and the song too. You're goated Uj, thanks for your work and dedication (edited)

一斗缶 2023/05/18 23:39
FLユーザーです。mp3で書き出しをしたら0dbを超えたのですが、これはしょうがない事なのでしょうか？ いつもはWAVで書き出しをしていて0dbを超えたことはありません。当然リミッターはかけてあります。
23:45
気になったのでbandcampで購入した音源から特に音圧が高いと感じているうじこさんのCherryでもWAVとmp3で比較を行なったのですが、 WAVではきっちりリミッティングされているのに対してmp3ではやはり0dbを超えていました。
23:49
0dbを超えているからか、mp3の方は少しざらついた様に聞こえるようなそうでもない様な気がするのですが、特に気にすることでは無いのでしょうか？ 長文ですみません。
sanmal (Staff) 2023/05/21 16:18
wav→mp3に変換する際の音量の乱れを見越して多めにヘッドルームを設けるか、Ozoneなどのトゥルーピークを抑えてくれるプラグインを使うかという感じですかね？ 以下のサイトも参考になりそうです https://ameblo.jp/pierottlunaire/entry-12237048991.html
『トゥルー・ピーク・リミッターとMP３エンコード考察』
最近改訂させて頂いたDTMマスタリングのやり方にトゥルー・ピーク・リミッターのことを少し書いたのですが、ブログでもうちょっと考察してみたいと思います。  昨今…
1
16:21

Ichigo Mysterium 2024/06/17 3:50
Well dang, I've been missing out on all this stuff! Been trying to get into making music recently and decided I'd check out the Patreon to check out the synth presets and whatnot just out of curiosity and checkout the workflow of my fave artists, but never thought about hopping on Discord tbh Just wanted to say I appreciate the hard work you've been doing @uj , and that PIXELIZE II was an absolute banger of an album! You inspired me to get into making music (Two songs out so far! But I need more practice.) and get into messing with synths and DAWs, so I'm super happy to become a Patron and return the favor :>
Ichigo Mysterium
Well dang, I've been missing out on all this stuff! Been trying to get into making music recently and decided I'd check out the Patreon to check out the synth presets and whatnot just out of curiosity and checkout the workflow of my fave artists, but never thought about hopping on Discord tbh Just wanted to say I appreciate the hard work you've been doing @uj , and that PIXELIZE II was an absolute banger of an album! You inspired me to get into making music (Two songs out so far! But I need more practice.) and get into messing with synths and DAWs, so I'm super happy to become a Patron and return the favor :>
uj 2024/06/17 4:19
hey! Thanks for the kind words, I'll move into new house with a studio on august and things are busy until then, but I'll make sure to make great content for people who aspire to become artists!
Ichigo Mysterium 2024/06/17 4:24
I wish you luck! Moving is a real pain, so much packing... and I appreciate that! Take your time though and enjoy your new place (⁠・⁠∀⁠・⁠) I love that first month or so when you look around and think "Damn, this is mine now??" Lol it's a great feeling
